name: New York City, New York

rent.notes: NYC rent stabilization may apply. Broker fees common (1 month rent). Most 2BR apartments in Brooklyn/Queens; Manhattan significantly higher.
rent.gigInternetNotes: Fios available in most of NYC with gig service. Some older buildings limited to Spectrum cable. Coverage varies by building.

groceries.notes: NYC grocery prices 20-30% above national average. Trader Joe's, Whole Foods, local bodegas and supermarkets. Delivery via FreshDirect, Instacart common.

electricity.notes: Con Edison. Higher summer A/C costs. NYC electricity rates among highest in US.

gas.notes: National Grid or Con Edison gas. Heating Oct-Apr. Many buildings include heat in rent.

water.notes: NYC Water Board. Often included in rent for apartments. Billed to building if not.

sewage.notes: NYC DEP. Billed with water, 159% of water charge.

trash.notes: NYC DSNY provides curbside pickup. Some buildings charge via maintenance fees.

utilities.taxes: NYC utility tax applies. Con Edison bills include various surcharges.
utilities.notes: Electricity, gas, water, sewage, trash. Many apartment buildings include heat and hot water in rent.

healthcare.notes: Medicare Part B ($174.70/mo) + Medigap Plan G (~$250/mo x2 people due to NYC premiums) + Part D (~$45/mo x2) + dental/vision plans. NYC Medigap premiums among highest in nation.

petCare.notes: NYC vet costs among highest in US. ASPCA, Banfield, and numerous private vets. Emergency vets: Animal Medical Center (24hr).

petDaycare.notes: Dog daycare 2 days/week, large breed rate (~$55-60/day). NYC premium pricing. Dog walking services $18-25 per 30-min walk.

petGrooming.notes: Professional grooming monthly. NYC grooming prices premium. Mobile groomers available.

transportation.notes: MTA subway/bus unlimited MetroCard $33/week or $132/mo. Many retirees car-free. If keeping car: insurance $250-400/mo, parking $300-600/mo, gas/tolls.

entertainment.notes: Incl cable+1Gig internet ($80), Netflix ($18), Amazon Prime ($15), Apple TV+ ($13), dining out (lunch for 2 2x/week w/ tax+tip ~$120/wk). Museums, Broadway, cultural events add up.

phoneCell.notes: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials. 2 phones, unlimited data. All taxes included.

miscellaneous.notes: Laundry services, building tips (holiday, super, doorman), NYC-specific fees and surcharges.

buffer.notes: Monthly safety buffer for unexpected expenses

medicalOOP.notes: Specialist visits, labs, procedures after Medicare. NYC specialist rates among highest in US. World-class facilities: NYU Langone, Mount Sinai, NewYork-Presbyterian.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household. EPIC program for NY seniors can reduce costs.

stateIncomeTax.exemptions: NY exempts Social Security from state income tax. Additional $20K pension exclusion for 59.5+. NYC city tax 3.078-3.876% additional.

cityIncomeTax.notes: NYC residents pay city income tax on top of NYS income tax.

salesTax.notes: NYC 8.875% (4% state + 4.5% city + 0.375% MTA surcharge). Clothing/shoes under $110 exempt.

propertyTax.notes: Renters: no direct property tax. NYC property tax rates high but borne by landlord.

taxes.notes: NYC has triple taxation: federal + NYS + NYC city income tax. SS exempt from NYS/NYC tax. STAR/SCHE property tax relief for senior homeowners.

healthcare.notes: World-class healthcare: NYU Langone, Mount Sinai, NewYork-Presbyterian, Memorial Sloan Kettering. EPIC program helps NY seniors with drug costs. Excellent specialist access.

lifestyle.notes: Central Park, Prospect Park, numerous dog runs. Unparalleled cultural scene: museums, Broadway, restaurants. Extremely diverse. Walkable city — car optional.

pros: World-class healthcare (NYU Langone, Mount Sinai, MSK)
Best public transit in US — car not needed
EPIC program for senior prescription assistance
cons: Highest cost of living in US
Triple taxation (federal + state + city income tax)
If keeping a car: insurance and parking extremely expensive
Healthcare premiums higher than national average
